curriculum.subject: Mathematics
curriculum.overallDescription: 6 units, 6 chapters, 13 topics, 0 subtopics, and 53 learning outcomes extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Identify reflexive, symmetric, transitive, and equivalence relations on sets.
Determine if a function is one-to-one (injective) or onto (surjective).
Define domain, range, and principal value branches of inverse trigonometric functions.
Sketch graphs of inverse trigonometric functions.
Classify matrices by type (zero, identity, square, rectangular) and property (symmetric, skew-symmetric).
Perform matrix addition, multiplication, and scalar multiplication with correct notation and order.
Demonstrate non-commutativity of matrix multiplication using examples.
Prove the uniqueness of the inverse for an invertible matrix.
Calculate minors, co-factors, adjoint, and inverse of a square matrix (up to 3x3).
Apply determinants to find the area of a triangle given vertex coordinates.
Determine consistency or inconsistency of systems of linear equations using determinant methods.
Solve systems of linear equations in two or three variables using the inverse of a matrix.

competency_groups.title: Relations and Functions
competency_groups.description: Understanding types of relations, equivalence classes, one-to-one and onto functions, inverse trigonometric functions including their domains, ranges, principal values, and graphs.
competencies: Identify reflexive, symmetric, transitive, and equivalence relations on sets.
Determine if a function is one-to-one (injective) or onto (surjective).
Define domain, range, and principal value branches of inverse trigonometric functions.
Sketch graphs of inverse trigonometric functions.

competency_groups.title: Algebra (Matrices)
competency_groups.description: Mastering matrix operations, properties of symmetric/skew-symmetric matrices, non-commutativity, invertibility, and uniqueness of inverses.
competencies: Classify matrices by type (zero, identity, square, rectangular) and property (symmetric, skew-symmetric).
Perform matrix addition, multiplication, and scalar multiplication with correct notation and order.
Demonstrate non-commutativity of matrix multiplication using examples.
Prove the uniqueness of the inverse for an invertible matrix.

competency_groups.title: Algebra (Determinants)
competency_groups.description: Calculating determinants of square matrices up to order 3, applying them to area problems, and solving systems of linear equations using matrix inverses.
competencies: Calculate minors, co-factors, adjoint, and inverse of a square matrix (up to 3x3).
Apply determinants to find the area of a triangle given vertex coordinates.
Determine consistency or inconsistency of systems of linear equations using determinant methods.
Solve systems of linear equations in two or three variables using the inverse of a matrix.

competency_groups.title: Calculus (Continuity and Differentiability)
competency_groups.description: Analyzing continuity, applying differentiation rules including chain rule for composite functions, implicit functions, parametric forms, logarithmic/exponential derivatives, and second-order derivatives.
competencies: Test the continuity of a function at a given point or over an interval.
Apply the chain rule to differentiate composite functions involving inverse trigonometric functions (sin^-1 x, cos^-1 x, tan^-1 x).
Differentiate implicit and parametrically defined functions.
Perform logarithmic differentiation for complex exponential/logarithmic functions.
Compute second-order derivatives of given functions.

competency_groups.title: Calculus (Applications of Derivatives)
competency_groups.description: Using derivatives to analyze rates of change, monotonicity, and extrema in theoretical and real-life contexts.
competencies: Calculate the rate of change of quantities with respect to time or other variables.
Determine intervals where a function is increasing or decreasing using first derivative tests.
Identify local maxima and minima using second derivative tests as provable tools.
Formulate and solve simple real-life optimization problems involving rates and extrema.

competency_groups.title: Calculus (Integrals)
competency_groups.description: Evaluating indefinite and definite integrals using substitution, partial fractions, integration by parts, and applying the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus.
competencies: Recognize integration as the inverse process of differentiation.
Evaluate standard integral forms involving x^2+a^2, a^2-x^2, ax+b, etc., using substitution or partial fractions.
Apply integration by parts to evaluate integrals of products of functions.
Use properties of definite integrals and the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus for evaluation.

competency_groups.title: Calculus (Application of Integrals)
competency_groups.description: Calculating areas under curves defined by lines, circles, parabolas, and ellipses in standard forms.
competencies: Set up definite integrals to find the area bounded by simple curves.
Calculate areas enclosed by lines, circles (standard form), parabolas (standard form), and ellipses (standard form).
Apply integration techniques to solve geometric area problems.

competency_groups.title: Calculus (Differential Equations)
competency_groups.description: Defining differential equations, determining order and degree, finding general/particular solutions via separation of variables, homogeneous methods, and linear forms.
competencies: Identify the order and degree of a given differential equation.
Find general and particular solutions for first-order differential equations using method of separation of variables.
Solve homogeneous differential equations of first order and first degree.
Solve linear differential equations of the form dy/dx + py = q or dx/dy + px = q.

competency_groups.title: Vectors (Fundamentals and Products)
competency_groups.description: Understanding vector properties, operations including dot and cross products, and their geometric interpretations.
competencies: Distinguish between vectors and scalars; determine magnitude and direction of a vector.
Calculate direction cosines and ratios for vectors in 2D or 3D space.
Perform vector addition and scalar multiplication operations.
Find the position vector of a point dividing a line segment internally in a given ratio.
Compute dot product (scalar) and cross product (vector) products, explaining their geometric interpretations.

competency_groups.title: Three-dimensional Geometry (Lines)
competency_groups.description: Deriving equations of lines in Cartesian and vector forms, analyzing relationships between skew lines, calculating shortest distances, and angles.
competencies: Determine direction cosines/ratios for a line joining two points.
Formulate the equation of a line in both Cartesian and Vector forms given specific conditions.
Identify if two lines are parallel or skew.
Calculate the shortest distance between two skew lines.

competency_groups.title: Geometry (Angles)
competency_groups.description: Calculating the angle between two lines in space.
competencies: Compute the acute angle between two given lines using their direction vectors or ratios.

competency_groups.title: Linear Programming (Fundamentals)
competency_groups.description: Defining linear programming terminology, constraints, objective functions, and identifying feasible/infeasible regions.
competencies: Define key terms: constraint, objective function, optimization in the context of Linear Programming Problems (LPP).
Formulate LPPs from word problems involving up to three non-trivial constraints.
Identify and distinguish between feasible/infeasible regions (bounded or unbounded) on a graph.

competency_groups.title: Linear Programming (Graphical Solution)
competency_groups.description: Solving LPPs in two variables using the graphical method to find optimal feasible solutions.
competencies: Plot constraints on a coordinate plane to determine the feasible region.
Identify corner points of the feasible region.
Evaluate the objective function at corner points to find optimal (maximum or minimum) values.

competency_groups.title: Probability (Foundations)
competency_groups.description: Understanding conditional probability, independence of events, and the multiplication theorem.
competencies: Calculate conditional probabilities P(A|B) for given events A and B.
Determine if two events are independent based on their joint and marginal probabilities.
Apply the multiplication theorem to find the probability of simultaneous occurrence of independent or dependent events.

competency_groups.title: Probability (Advanced Theorems)
competency_groups.description: Applying the total probability theorem and Bayes' theorem to solve complex probabilistic problems.
competencies: Calculate probabilities using the Total Probability Theorem for partitioned sample spaces.
Apply Bayes' Theorem to find posterior probabilities given prior information and new evidence.

topics: Continuity and Differentiability: Continuity and differentiability, chain rule, derivative of composite functions, derivatives of inverse trigonometric functions like sin^-1 x, cos^-1 x and tan^-1 x, derivative of implicit functions. Concept of exponential and logarithmic functions. Derivatives of logarithmic and exponential functions. Logarithmic differentiation, derivative of functions expressed in parametric forms. Second order derivatives.

Applications of Derivatives: Applications of derivatives: rate of change of quantities, increasing/decreasing functions, maxima and minima (first derivative test motivated geometrically and second derivative test given as a provable tool). question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.
Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria.
Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

bloom_levels: Remembering: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.

Applying: Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Analysing: Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations
